mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer spending that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in the unemployment rate alongside an elevated federal funds rate above 4% signal stagflationary conditions, where tight policy intersects with weakening labor income to depress corporate earnings and generate negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over prior 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 4%
regime_hint: tight policy with deteriorating labor market
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE changes and FEDFUNDS levels adds no incremental predictive power, with marginal R-squared below 0.01 in quarterly regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production exceeding 1% over a three-month window marks a supply contraction that reduces corporate output and margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and wage income that supports aggregate demand and corporate profitability, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1400 thousand units indicate strong residential investment and durable goods demand that propagates through the economy, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of quarters when housing starts exceed 1400 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy expectations, raising real discount rates and eroding purchasing power, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP captures the aggregate output expansion that underlies broad-based revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, forecasting positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
